cls:oxide.name: Oxides
cls:oxide.description: Binary compounds of an element with oxygen

cls:oxide_basic.name: Basic oxides
cls:oxide_basic.description: Metal oxides that react with acids to form a salt and water

cls:oxide_acidic.name: Acidic oxides
cls:oxide_acidic.description: Nonmetal oxides that react with bases to form a salt and water

cls:oxide_amphoteric.name: Amphoteric oxides
cls:oxide_amphoteric.description: Oxides that exhibit both basic and acidic properties

cls:oxide_indifferent.name: Indifferent oxides
cls:oxide_indifferent.description: Oxides that do not form salts with acids or bases

cls:acid.name: Acids
cls:acid.description: Compounds composed of hydrogen atoms and an acid residue

cls:acid_oxygen.name: Oxyacids
cls:acid_oxygen.description: Acids whose acid residue contains oxygen

cls:acid_oxygenfree.name: Binary acids
cls:acid_oxygenfree.description: Acids whose acid residue does not contain oxygen

cls:base.name: Bases
cls:base.description: Compounds composed of a metal cation and hydroxide anion OH⁻

cls:base_alkali.name: Alkalis
cls:base_alkali.description: Water-soluble bases formed by alkali and alkaline earth metals

cls:base_insoluble.name: Insoluble bases
cls:base_insoluble.description: Bases insoluble in water, decompose on heating

cls:base_amphoteric.name: Amphoteric bases
cls:base_amphoteric.description: Bases that exhibit both basic and acidic properties

cls:salt.name: Salts
cls:salt.description: Compounds composed of a metal cation and an acid residue anion

cls:salt_normal.name: Normal salts
cls:salt_normal.description: Salts in which all hydrogen atoms of the acid are replaced by a metal

cls:salt_acidic.name: Acidic salts
cls:salt_acidic.description: Salts in which not all hydrogen atoms of the acid are replaced by a metal

cls:salt_basic.name: Basic salts
cls:salt_basic.description: Salts in which not all hydroxyl groups of the base are replaced by an acid residue

prop:state_of_matter.name: State of matter
prop:state_of_matter.description: The form in which a substance exists, determined by the motion and interaction of its particles: solid, liquid, gas, plasma. Water is a classic example: ice (solid), liquid water, steam (gas).

prop:color.name: Color
prop:color.description: A property determined by the wavelengths of photons a substance reflects or emits. Depends on electronic structure: Cu(OH)₂ is blue, Fe(OH)₃ is brown, KMnO₄ is purple.

prop:odor.name: Odor
prop:odor.description: A property perceived by the sense of smell, determined by volatile molecules interacting with olfactory receptors. H₂S smells of rotten eggs; NH₃ has a sharp, pungent smell.

prop:density.name: Density
prop:density.description: Mass per unit volume of a substance (g/cm³ or kg/m³). Determines whether a substance sinks or floats, and depends on particle mass and packing efficiency.

prop:melting_point.name: Melting point
prop:melting_point.description: The temperature at which a substance transitions from solid to liquid at a given pressure. Depends on crystal lattice type and strength of interparticle bonds.

prop:boiling_point.name: Boiling point
prop:boiling_point.description: The temperature at which a substance's vapor pressure equals the external pressure and it transitions to a gaseous state. Water boils at 100 °C (at 1 atm).

prop:thermal_stability.name: Thermal stability
prop:thermal_stability.description: The ability of a substance to maintain its chemical composition and structure when heated. Insoluble bases and some salts (CaCO₃) decompose on heating; alkali hydroxides are thermally stable.

prop:flammability.name: Flammability
prop:flammability.description: The ability of a substance to undergo exothermic oxidation releasing heat and light (combustion). Depends on substance nature, ignition temperature, and oxidizer availability.

prop:atom.name: Atom
prop:atom.description: The smallest particle of a chemical element that retains its chemical properties. Consists of a positively charged nucleus (protons + neutrons) and an electron shell. Size is on the order of 10⁻¹⁰ m.

prop:molecule.name: Molecule
prop:molecule.description: An electrically neutral particle formed by two or more atoms joined by covalent bonds. The smallest particle of a substance that retains its chemical properties. H₂O, CO₂, O₂ are molecules.

prop:ion.name: Ion
prop:ion.description: An atom or group of atoms carrying an electric charge as a result of losing or gaining electrons. Cation (+) — loss of electrons; anion (−) — gain of electrons.

prop:crystal_lattice.name: Crystal lattice
prop:crystal_lattice.description: An ordered three-dimensional arrangement of particles (atoms, molecules, or ions) in a solid. The lattice type (ionic, atomic, molecular, metallic) determines physical properties: melting point, hardness, brittleness.

prop:oxidizer.name: Oxidizing agent
prop:oxidizer.description: An atom, molecule, or ion that accepts electrons in a redox reaction. The oxidation state of the oxidizing agent decreases (it is reduced).

prop:reducer.name: Reducing agent
prop:reducer.description: An atom, molecule, or ion that donates electrons in a redox reaction. The oxidation state of the reducing agent increases (it is oxidized).
